questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct about the Chilenito?1) It is a critically endangered species of cactus.2) It is endemic to Chile.3) Its scientific name is Eriosyce chilensis.Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The scientific name of Chilenito is Eriosyce chilensis. They are endemic to Chile. It is a critically endangered species of cactus. According to the IUCN, this plant is one of the world’s 100 most threatened species.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding Spoon-billed sandpiper?1) Its scientific name is Eurynorhyncus pygmeus.2) The most distinctive feature of this species is its spatulate bill.3) The IUCN status of Spoon-billed sandpiper is Critically Endangered.Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The scientific name of Spoon-billed sandpiper is Eurynorhyncus pygmeus. The IUCN status of the Spoon-billed sandpiper is Critically endangered. The most distinctive feature of this species is its spatulate bill.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding Table mountain ghost frog?1) Its scientific name is Heleophryne rosei.2) It is listed as critically endangered by IUCN.3) It is endemic to the western slopes of Table Mountain in Cape Town.Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The Table mountain ghost frog belongs to the family Heleophrynidae. It has a very restricted range and it is endemic to the southern, eastern, and marginally western slopes of Table Mountain in Cape Town. It is listed as critically endangered by IUCN.

questions.question: The ‘Aichi Targets’ were adopted by which of the following?
questions.explanation: The COP-10 which was held at Nagoya, Japan gave birth to two things:1) Nagoya Protocol on Genetic Resources2) Aichi Targets for biodiversity. The ‘Aichi Target’ was adopted by the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) at its Nagoya conference.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the world in terms of plant diversity?
questions.explanation: In terms of plant diversity, India ranks tenth in the world and fourth in Asia. It is concentrated in four phytogeographically unique regions, viz., Himalayas, Western Ghats, Northeast India, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: Which of the following salts is the main cause of permanent hardness of water?
questions.explanation: The simple definition of water hardness is the amount of dissolved calcium and magnesium in the water. Permanent hardness in water is due to the presence of the chlorides, nitrates and sulphates of calcium and magnesium mainly Magnesium sulphate.

questions.question: International Tropical Timber Organization was established in which of the following year?
questions.explanation: The International Tropical Timber Agreement also called as ITTA was signed in 1983. International Tropical Timber Organization was established under the International Tropical Timber Agreement in 1983 and entered into force in 1985.

questions.question: Department of Environment was established in India in which of the following years?
questions.explanation: In the year 1980 the Department of Environment was established in India which was to ensure a healthy environment for the country. This department later became the Ministry of Environment and Forests (MoEF) in 1985.

questions.question: European Union Emissions Trading System was launched in which of the following year?
questions.explanation: The European Union Emissions Trading System (EU ETS), was the first large greenhouse gas emissions trading scheme in the world. It was launched in the year 2005 to fight global warming.

questions.question: The concept of sustainable development was described by which of the following?
questions.explanation: The concept of sustainable development was described by the Bruntland Commission Report of 1987. It described sustainable development as “development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.”
